REPLY MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-29-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 ------------------------ AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. IN LOOKING AT THE TERMS OF THE POLICY, THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE 3RD PARTY'S CROSS-COMPLAINT AND ALL FACTS KNOWN TO DEFENDANT FROM ANY SOURCE TO DETERMINE COVERAGE, THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT HAS MAINTAINED ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. THE DOCUMENTS INDICATE THERE WAS NO "OCCURRENCE" AND THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S INTENTIONAL ACTS. PLAINTIFF'S RELIANCE ON ALLSTATE AND GRAY IS MISPLACED. THEY ARE NOT APPLICABLE TO THIS CASE. "OUR SUPREME COURT ANTICIPATING IMAGINATIVE COUNSEL AND THE LIKELIHOOD OF ARTFUL DRAFTING, HAS INDICATED THAT A THIRD PARTY IS NOT THE ARBITER OF THE POLICY'S COVERAGE. A COROLLARY TO THIS RULE IS THAT THE INSURED MAY NOT SPECULATE ABOUT UNPLED THIRD PARTY CLAIMS TO MANUFACTURE COVERAGE." HURLEY CONSTRUCTION CO. V. STATE FARM FIRE AND CASUALTY CO., (1992) 10 CAL.APP. 4TH 533, 538. THE COURT SIGNED DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER. COUNSEL WERE NOTIFIED OF THE RULING BY TELEPHONE.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF HUI, CHI KIN TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE ON THE COMPLAINT, SAID DEFENDANT TO HAVE JUDGMENT THEREON FOR $910.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $910.00, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT
NOTICE OF APPEAL/REQUEST FOR TRANSCRIPT (NTC OF APPEAL ONLY) FILED BY PLAINTIFF HUI, CHI KIN
MEMORANDUM OF COSTS AND DISBURSEMENTS, $494.00 TOTAL COSTS, MATURE DATE AUG-23-2012, FILED BY DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE

NOTICE OF MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE ON OCT-21-2011 AT 3:00 PM IN DEPARTMENT 504. NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR NOV-07-2011 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON FEB-04-2011 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
NOTICE OF EARLY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE - EARLY SETTLEMENT PROGRAM

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHI KIN HUI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR PLAINTIFF ON ISSUE OF DUTY TO DEFEND AND FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE RECORD IS DEVOID OF FACTS SHOWING A POSSIBILITY OF COVERAGE. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN TO DEFENDANT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. COURT RULED ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-13-2011 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Anthony D. Giles of Logan & Giles, LLP (925.945.6167) for plaintiff and responding party; Lori A. Schweitzer and Julie Williams of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer (510.658.3600) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
------------------------------------------------
After hearing, the court ruled as follows: defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. In looking at the terms of the policy, the allegations of the 3rd party's cross-complaint and all facts known to defendant from any source to determine coverage, the court finds that defendant has maintained its summary judgment burden. The documents indicate there was no "occurrence" and the cross-complaint is based on plaintiff's intentional acts. Plaintiff's reliance on Allstate and Gray is misplaced. They are not applicable to this case. "Our Supreme Court anticipating imaginative counsel and the likelihood of artful drafting, has indicated that a third party is not the arbiter of the policy's coverage. A corollary to this rule is that the insured may not speculate about unpled third party claims to manufacture coverage." Hurley Construction Co. v. State Farm Fire and Casualty Co., (1992) 10 Cal.App. 4th 533, 538. The court signed defendant's proposed order. Counsel were notified of the ruling by telephone.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF CHI KIN HUI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR PLAINTIFF ON ISSUE OF DUTY TO DEFEND AND FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF.

APPEARANCES: Anthony D. Giles, Esq. 925/945-6167 & William A. Logan, Jr., Esq. 925/945-6792 of Logan & Giles, LLP for pltf & moving party; Dolores Bastian Dalton, Esq. of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer 510/334-5822 for deft & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE RECORD IS DEVOID OF FACTS SHOWING A POSSIBILITY OF COVERAGE. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN TO DEFENDANT.
PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.
Court ruled on defendant's objections to evidence, order signed.
(302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
